2603.19688.title: DataProphet: Demystifying Supervision Data Generalization in Multimodal LLMs
2603.19688.paper_summary: Conventional wisdom for selecting supervision data for multimodal large language models (MLLMs) is to prioritize datasets that appear similar to the target benchmark, such as text-intensive or vision-centric tasks. However, it remains unclear whether such intuitive similarity reliably predicts downstream performance gains. In this work, we take a first step toward answering a practical question: can we estimate the influence of a training dataset on a target benchmark before any training is performed? To investigate this question, we conduct an in-depth analysis of transfer across 14 vision-language datasets spanning 7 diverse tasks. Our results show that intuitive task similarity is an unreliable predictor of transferability, and that generalization depends more on the specific dataset than on its broad task category. Motivated by this finding, we propose DATAPROPHET, a simple and effective training-free metric that combines multimodal perplexity, similarity, and data diversity. Experiments show that DATAPROPHET produces supervision-data rankings that strongly correlate with rankings based on actual post-training performance gains, achieving a Kendall's tau of 86.0%. Moreover, DATAPROPHET enables better supervision-data selection, yielding up to 6.9% improvement over uniform selection, 1.4% over a state-of-the-art training-based baseline, and 0.2% above oracle selection based on experimental performance. Our code and data will be released.
2603.19688.paper_summary_zh: 傳統智慧在選擇多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）的監督數據時，是優先考慮與目標基準相似的數據集，例如文本密集或以視覺為中心的任務。然而，這種直觀的相似性是否可靠地預測下游性能提升仍不清楚。在這項工作中，我們邁出了回答一個實際問題的第一步：我們能否在進行任何訓練之前估計訓練數據集對目標基準的影響？為了探討這個問題，我們對跨越7個多樣任務的14個視覺-語言數據集進行了深入分析。我們的結果顯示，直觀的任務相似性並不是可轉移性的可靠預測指標，而是泛化更多依賴於特定數據集而非其廣泛的任務類別。受到這一發現的啟發，我們提出了DATAPROPHET，一種簡單且有效的無訓練度量，結合了多模態困惑度、相似性和數據多樣性。實驗顯示，DATAPROPHET產生的監督數據排名與基於實際訓練後性能提升的排名強烈相關，達到86.0%的Kendall's tau。此外，DATAPROPHET能夠更好地選擇監督數據，相較於均勻選擇提高了最多6.9%，相較於最先進的基於訓練的基準提高了1.4%，以及相較於基於實驗性能的oracle選擇提高了0.2%。我們的代碼和數據將會發布。
2603.19688.author: Xuan Qi et.al.
2603.19688.authors: Xuan Qi, Luxi He, Dan Roth, Xingyu Fu
